results.title: UM to transfer 4,000 students to Hengqin campus by 2029
results.description: The University of Macau’s (UM) Hengqin campus project is expected to be fully completed by 2029, when it will accommodate around 4,000 students, rector Yonghua Song said yesterday during a media visit by the “Opportunities in Macau” delegation. Speaking to the press, he added that nearly 1,000 postgraduate students will begin studying at the Guangdong-Macao In-Depth Cooperation Zone’s Macau–Hengqin International Education (University) Town transitional campus as early as August this year. Song said the Hengqin project is a key initiative under China’s 15th Five-Year Plan support framework and will adopt an “extension-based” model, under which UM will not establish a [...] The post UM to transfer 4,000 students to Hengqin campus by 2029 appeared first on MACAU DAILY TIMES 澳門每日時報 .

results.title: Indian-American Faces Citizenship Revocation Over Fraud Allegations
results.description: A 54-year-old Indian-American man is facing a serious lawsuit from the US government, which could lead to the revocation of his citizenship. Allegations include obtaining immigration benefits through identity fraud and misrepresentation. The case highlights the complexities of immigration law and the potential consequences of fraudulent actions. Singh, who initially applied for immigration benefits in 1990, is accused of concealing his deportation history and providing false information during his naturalisation process. If the court rules against him, he could face denaturalisation and deportation. This case underscores the importance of honesty in immigration applications and the legal ramifications of deceit.

results.title: Travel Manitoba says it’s business as usual despite warning issued by U.S. agency
results.description: Travel Manitoba says it has received no questions or concerns from American tourists about a recent U.S. travel alert concerning the hepatitis A outbreak in Manitoba.

results.title: Vedanta Aluminium vs Power vs Oil & Gas vs Iron & Steel: Which stock should you buy?

Analysts advise patience, focusing on business quality over price action. Experts suggest aluminium offers the best risk-reward for long-term investors due to strong demand and cost efficiencies.

results.title: Charlotte Hall Man Charged With Assault, Wearing a Handgun While Under the Influence
results.description: Charles Edward Karpiak, 57, of Charlotte Hall, was arrested on June 17, 2026, and charged with first-degree assault, second-degree assault, and wearing a handgun while under the influence. The charges were filed in District Court in St. Mary’s County following an arrest by the St. Mary’s County Sheriff’s Office. Karpiak is accused of committing the offenses on June 17, 2026. The most serious charge, first-degree assault, is listed as a felony. Karpiak also faces misdemeanor charges of second-degree assault and wearing a handgun while under the influence. After his initial appearance on June 17, 2026, Karpiak was ordered held without bond. He also waived an attorney at the initial appearance, according to the case information. A bail or bond review hearing is scheduled for June 18, 2026, at 1:00 p.m. in Courtroom 2 at St. Mary’s District Court. At the time of publication, additional documents outlining the specific allegations in the case were not available. This article is based on the case information that was publicly available as of June 17, 2026. We will provide an update as more details become available. The post Charlotte Hall Man Charged With Assault, Wearing a Handgun While Under the Influence appeared first on Southern Maryland News Net .

results.title: Trump Signs Historic Iran Memorandum at Versailles, Ushering in New Diplomatic Era
results.description: In a landmark event, President Donald J. Trump has signed the Iran Memorandum of Understanding at the Palace of Versailles, signaling a new phase in US-Iran relations. This agreement, which follows a digital endorsement by senior officials, aims to de-escalate tensions and foster diplomatic engagement. The signing ceremony represents a culmination of extensive negotiations and is expected to guide future discussions on regional stability and security. As global observers closely monitor the situation, further details on the implementation of the memorandum are anticipated soon.

results.title: In Pics: Alexander Zverev’s daughter shares special moment with girlfriend Sophia Thomalla at Halle Open
results.description: Alexander Zverev’s daughter Mayla was seen in public during his Halle Open first-round match, sitting with his girlfriend Sophia Thomalla in the player box. Zverev defeated Vit Kopriva in three sets to reach the second round. The German star will next face Yannick Hanfmann as he continues his bid for a first Halle Open title on home soil.
